All right, welcome back. So what you just saw was like the duct tape and bubble gum stuck together version of our current super system. Now, what's really cool is that thing is actually working very, very well. Check this out. So I'm recording this for you on the 10th of August. And if you look over here, these are our Facebook ad stats. And some of these are just intent-based branding videos, which we'll talk about much later. But these are ads that are designed to get a sales. And you'll see we've got a couple of campaigns where there's zero clicks and there's zero spend, but there's profit. And you see, you'd be like, well, how could there possibly be revenue here? Well, the reason why is because these folks came into that super system weeks ago while the campaigns were running. I've since turned these campaigns off, but I still got sales, and it shows the sales today. That's how I have my tracking set up. But you can see right here, I've brought in $300 and whatever that is, $359. Is that the right math? Yeah, I think it is $359. Just from my follow-up system, just from people who had clicked on these little campaigns like a week or so ago, which is bonkers. So super systems really, really work. They're very, very good to have. This is the stuff that makes your ads work better by far. So the bubblegum duct tape version that we're running now is mainly just selling info products. And I just threw it together because something is better than nothing. Now, the one I'm going to show you next is one that I built back when we were selling our Sandor certification. I will likely build something similar to sell this very program. Sandor certification was $18,000. It was sold via a phone consult, as you could imagine. And when you're selling something via a phone consult, you're really making the same offer like a million times, which is, I've got this thing. Do you want to get on the phone and talk about it? And a lot of people that have phone funnels, they quit really, really soon. They're like, well, I ran a webinar or whatever. And I invited people to get on a phone call. And this many people showed up. And we made this much money. And it's pretty good or whatever. Here's how to turbocharge the whole thing. Now, it doesn't matter if you're running a phone system or not. What I want you to do is watch this and realize how hardcore you can be with email follow-up. The more stuff that we put between get a lead and sell them something, the more little processes we've got in there, the more sell them something that we're going to get. So without further ado, here is a version of one that I built when we were really building it in pristine fashion. And I built this one on an infusion soft. Of course, like I said earlier, I use Active Campaign now, mainly because I like the email deliverability better on Active Campaign. And I think their builder thingy is easier to use. But it's six, one, half dozen the other. You use whatever you want to use. Like I said earlier in the last video, we have templates for quick sequences, for countdown sequences. We have those automation templates we can just give you. What you're about to see is exactly like any super system in the world. It's a bunch of quick sequences and countdown sequences duct taped together to make one big ass super sequence. That's all this is. But this one is really, really bad ass. So without further ado, let's get it. All right. So let's say this is someone's campaign. You have a client. And they're, let's say it's you. You're selling a course or whatever, or a client. So it doesn't matter, whoever. And they're getting traffic to an on demand webinar or automated webinar. It doesn't matter. And they're buying ads that send people to a registration page. And another way they're driving traffic is they have an email list. And they're inviting people from their email list. We make this a little bigger for us. There we go. I don't want to do that. OK, there we go. All right. So the ad, they click on the ad, they register, they get tagged. Now it looks to see if they attend. OK, so now we have registered. Webinar number one, registered. That tag is applied. If they attend, webinar number one, attended, that tag is applied. Let's say you're sending them to the invite sequence from your email. And you want them to re-register for whatever reason. Well, if they register, then they get tagged. Webinar number one, registered. If they don't register, they get tagged. Webinar number one, did not register. And in this case, if you're sending them an invite sequence from your email and they don't register, well, they're out of the campaign. So what do you do? You tag them as, hey, they didn't register. And you send them to the super system. I'll show you the super system diagram in a minute. But what I'm really trying to show you right now is all of the possible exit points. So right here, this is an exit point. Now if we have built a webinar system perfectly, then we don't immediately send them to the super system if they don't attend. Instead, we put them in a go finish sequence. And so if you'll refer back to on demand training sequences and automated webinar sequences, you'll see how we have that whole go finish, the thing you started, sequence built. So we tag them as, hey, they didn't attend. And then we put them in the go finish sequences. It has nothing to do with the super system. This is just normal, normal course of this campaign, not even involving the super system yet. And let's say now it's looking to say, OK, they've been through the sequence. Did they see the pitch? Well, if they registered and they didn't attend, they're tagged. If they did not see the pitch, they're tagged as, still tagged as registered webinar one, still tagged as attended webinar number one. And now they're tagged as left early or did not see pitch, however you want to do it for webinar one. And in this case, we've got an exit point. So all the way down, boom, send them to the super system. Into the super system you go. But let's say they do attend and they do see the pitch. Well, OK, they're tagged as attended. They're tagged as saw the pitch. And now we're going to look to see if they bought. Super simple. They're also tagged here if they saw the pitch. So now we look to see if they bought. If they bought, they're out of the super system. We don't need to be sending them to another webinar for the same product or whatever. So you can put them into a backend system or you can put them into a campaign for a separate product. It's really up to you. But in this case, we're looking to see, OK, they didn't buy. So now they would be tagged as saw pitch, did not buy. Now if we're doing it perfectly, remember last week, we got our triple stack deadline. So of course, we would be terrible people if we had a client with a webinar sequence or an on-demand training sequence or a VSLC, like any campaign, and we don't bolt on a triple stack deadline sequence. We're crazy. So they didn't buy. We tag them. We put them through deadline sequence number one. They don't buy. And so we tag them. Deadline sequence number one. Webinar one, deadline sequence one, did not buy. We wait 48 hours. And then we put them in deadline sequence number two. And then if they bought or if they don't buy, well, again, we tag them. Webinar number one. Saw deadline sequence number two, did not buy. And we do the same thing with webinar deadline sequence number three. So this is from our deadline stack. So at the end of all of this, we're tagging them for every stage if they did the thing or if they didn't. And ultimately, if they don't buy, we send them to the super system. Now, there's one tag we also need to put. And been through webinar campaign number one. Okay, just so you know, because like if the person comes here and they register and they don't attend and we don't tag them as having been through campaign number one, then when it looks to say, hey, have they been through campaign number one and they don't have that tag, it's just gonna send them right back to the same campaign. So we're going, okay, someone leaves a campaign, they enter the super system. All it's gonna do is it'd be like, hey, have they been through webinar number one yet? If no, boom, it sends them here. And where would it send them? Well, because we've already got their email address, it would send them to the invite sequence, right? And so they would get tagged, been through webinar number one, so we don't resend them to that sequence unnecessarily. And then it would look and it would deploy this sequence, right? Let's say they have been through webinar number one. So it takes like seconds for these decisions to be met, by the way, in any automation system. But now we're looking to see if they registered, because we can send somebody through an invite sequence, but they're not all going to register, are they? So if they have the tag, been through webinar number one, but they don't have the tag registered for webinar number one, what do we do? We're like, oh, okay, let's send them back through that click sequence to get them to register. So we apply a tag, start webinar number one click sequence, and that, blammo, fires this off again. Send them the same email sequence, and you might be like, well, dude, we just send them that email sequence. It seems that way on paper, but in reality, the only possible way they can even get to this phase is if they've been through all of these already. So if each one of, just the invite sequence is alone, if your invite sequence is two emails a day for three days, and there's like a 24 or 48 hour wait period before you put them into the super system here at the end, so let's say the register is no, and there's a timer, wait two days, then send to the super system, that means this whole thing is gonna take them three plus two, so that's five, 10, 15 days before they even get here, right? The fastest and simplest thing is just to send someone back through the invite sequence. And so it's like, okay, we've filtered every imaginal behavioral thing. Did they register? Did they attend? Did they see the pitch? You know, if the answer is yes to all of this and they still haven't bought, we could either start trying to sell them something else, send them affiliate offers, whatever it might be. But usually what you do with a client is once we build one of these systems for them, the client then starts just adding new campaigns. So now what they would do is they'd be like, oh, I just messed that up. Didn't mean to do that. Okay, now what they would do is they would just put a webinar number four here at the top, you know? And so you can keep bolting on campaigns to a super system like that. So let me show you some real life examples from my own campaign. And again, here's what's really, really important. From the tech side, it doesn't, you personally do not need to understand on a technical level how all of this granular tagging and decision diamond stuff works. You need to be able to hand this to your client or a version of this to sketch it out for them and say, give this to your tech person and let's deploy it. And remember, your client's going to have a tech person because we're only doing super system architecture for people who have multiple funnels. And you ain't gonna have multiple funnels if you don't know how to do tech stuff, right? So just keep that in mind and don't freak yourself out in terms of, oh my God, I don't know how to filter out all of these people you ain't supposed to. That's their tech guy's job. Like I don't build this stuff for people. My clients that I advise to do this for, I didn't build it for them. I said, all right, man, here's what you need to do. And they did it and they made a lot of money as a result. It's awesome. And I've done it and made a lot of money as a result. And that too was awesome. Okay, let me find you some real life examples. Here we are. This is my Infusionsoft account and this is how I've built a super system. I like to call them decision machines sometimes because it just sounds funny to me, same thing. And this is how I built a super system, AKA decision machine for three different webinars that all were offering, hey, if you're interested in certification, fill this little thingy out and we'll talk to you, okay? So it all starts where the tag is applied, start the Sandor decision machine, okay? And how does that tag get applied? It gets applied here, right? And so if someone like doesn't register for the webinar or whatever and they don't do what we want them to do, whether it's here or over here or whatever, it waits a couple of days and it says, okay, start the decision machine. So that tag gets applied. And when it does, the first thing we did is I checked, actually, this is a little placeholder leading to a decision diamond. Again, like I don't expect you to do all of this tech stuff. I'm just kind of showing it to you. And if your clients need to show their tech people, it's totally cool with me if you show them this little segment here. So what it would do is it would be like, okay, let's make sure that someone hasn't already filled out a type form to talk to us, right? So if they have never filled out that type form, remember what powers a super system tags, right? We tag everything. So someone hasn't filled out the type form. That's the equivalent of them buying in this case. That's our ultimate goal. Then we're like, okay, cool, let's keep going. So what we do now is we remove that same tag that started this. The reason why is because if it's already there and we go to reapply it, it won't start everything. So we just take it away. That way, at the end of one of the webinar campaigns, when we reapply it, it'll fire everything again. All right? If they have already filled out the type form, we take them out of the super system. We're not messing with them. They're already in the different phase, right? So the whole purpose of the webinars for Sandor was to get people to be like, yes, I wanna talk to you. I'm interested in getting certified. So if they've already done that, then we don't need to mess with them anymore, okay? So we remove this little tag. That just gives us the ability to start the campaign over. And now it's just gonna look for stuff. And what it's gonna look for is it's gonna say, okay, does the tag have this, I'm trying to get this actual tag name. Does the tag have been through Sandor decision machine or not, okay? And if it does, that means it's been through everything. Okay, so at the end of this particular super system that I built, all the way over here, when they finally get to the end, I give them a tag that says been through everything. Right? And so if they've got that tag, I'm looking all the way over here at the top. Right here. All right, if they have it, I'm not gonna put them through anything else. They've been through everything, right? So in your system, you might apply that tag here, been through everything. Okay, and the reason we wanna apply that tag is because somebody can enter the super system at the end of webinar one, webinar two, webinar three. Like it's not linear, you know? So they could come into the super system like in all kinds of different super circumstances. So you just wanna make sure that you're not constantly looping people through the same stuff. What that's going to do is, it's gonna make this sequence fire right here for automated webinar number one, because if they've already got the tag, at least in infusion soft, and you reapply it, nothing happens. So now they get back over here, okay? So it's like, oh, man, so what we can do is this little guy right here is looking to see if they ever attended, if they have not attended, I can, I'm gonna, I've got a program to tell them to go back. You know, I'm gonna re-invite them again. If they have attended, then I'm like, okay, we're not gonna send them back to Freedom Multiplier. So there's nothing here. And then now it's gonna check, it's gonna be like, all right, well, have they been through? This was a video, this wasn't even an automated webinar. This was a video here. Have they been through the overview from Decision Machine? So have I sent them here before from my super system? Decision Machine and super system mean the same thing. I like making up multiple words for things to be very confusing. So it's gonna look, right? And if they have not, then it's gonna check and it's gonna be like, okay, well, we've never sent them here from the Decision Machine, but maybe because that overview was built in to my blueprint for every single one of these webinars. So instead of like a deadline sequence, because I'm trying to get them to fill out a type form, I follow up with other content, you know? So that overview goes here, because in Sandor, there's not like, hey, you have 48 hours or they give you more bonuses. It's more like, all right, here's an overview and here's more info, all right? So it's gonna look. And if they haven't been through there, then I'm gonna send them to that overview video. If they have, then I'm gonna skip it. And now I'm going to look over here and my Decision Diamond is gonna be like, okay, well, they have not been through the update here, all right? So that's fine, all right, whatever. So now we're gonna look again. So overview and update are different, right? So overview is one piece of content that talks about how cool Sandor is. Overview is another one, right? So instead of an automated webinar, this is just a VSL basically, and this really kind of shows you that it doesn't have to be three of the same types of campaigns. So it's like, all right, have they ever sent them through that campaign from here before? If not, I'm gonna send them here, you know? I'm gonna be like, hey, go check out this overview thingy. And if I have, all right, then they end up here. They've been through everything, right? And so this is like a classic super system that I built for Sandor. And this is why Sandor worked so well. I had all these assets and if people dropped out of any of them, I'm selling the exact same product. I'm just using multiple things. I got two automated webinars and then two like overview and overview videos and then an update video, all of them making the same pitch, hey, go apply for type form. So this made it where once somebody came in, whether they registered for one of these things from Facebook or from my list or something, once they got, once they exited any of these campaigns, now they're in this system. And all I was doing is following this architecture right here, you know? And so it doesn't matter if it's a webinar, if it's a video sales letter, if it's an automated launch, this is how you architect this stuff, right? And so I hope you found this helpful.
